draft.introduction: The Yoga Vasishtha is an Advaita Vedanta philosophical text framed as the sage Vasishtha’s teachings to the young prince Rama. Book 2 (Mumukshu Prakarana) examines the nature of dispassion and the longing for liberation.

translation.text: Rama said: ‘O sage, this world appears real but upon investigation proves to be unreal, like a dream. Tell me how the mind creates this illusion.’ Vasishtha replied: ‘The world is like a long dream. It appears real as long as ignorance persists. When self-knowledge arises, it dissolves like mist before the sun.’
translation.original_text: राम उवाच। भगवन् इदं जगत् सत्यं प्रतीयते

translator_notes.note: The Yoga Vasishtha’s comparison of the world to a dream anticipates Berkeley’s idealism and shares ground with Buddhist “mind-only” philosophy. Its teaching that liberation is available here and now through understanding—not after death—makes it one of the most radical texts in the Advaita tradition.
